beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and grab coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Derek's typical 5:30am wake for a 6am work start; full energy on Monday morning.

beats.activity: Drive from Renton to Bellevue construction site.
beats.reasoning: 40 min drive from Renton to Bellevue, arriving before 7am to supervise morning crew setup.

beats.activity: Stop at local diner for breakfast and check construction plans.
beats.reasoning: Derek's routine: diner breakfast before site, sports radio habit fits early Monday morning.

beats.activity: Walk back to Turner Construction site from diner.
beats.reasoning: Short walk from diner to site; Derek reviews crew assignments on arrival.

beats.activity: Supervise construction crew on-site: frame inspection, material coordination, safety checks.
beats.reasoning: Core work block; Derek is a foreman, fully engaged in morning operations until the USA–Wales match at 11am.

beats.activity: Step away from site to catch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match at a downtown sports bar.
beats.reasoning: The USA–Wales match airs at exactly 11am; Derek is a sports fan and Bellevue has several bars with live coverage—he takes a 30 min break to watch the opening.

beats.activity: Return to Turner Construction site from sports bar.
beats.reasoning: 20 min walk/drive back to site; Derek refocuses on crew oversight after a quick match glimpse.

beats.activity: Continue work: coordinate concrete pour, safety briefing, equipment maintenance.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon work block, full focus on crew productivity; Light Rail closure 9am–3pm doesn't affect Derek's car commute.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a sandwich and hydrate in the sunny weather.
beats.reasoning: Early June sunshine (74F high) calls for a quick outdoor lunch; Derek stays hydrated and fueled for afternoon site work.

beats.activity: Return to Turner Construction site from lunch.
beats.reasoning: 15 min walk back to site; Derek heads back to final afternoon push.

beats.activity: Final work block: inspect framing progress, wrap up crew assignments, close out daily log.
beats.reasoning: Derek works through mid-afternoon; typical foreman shift wind-down, preparing crew for next day.

beats.activity: Drive from Bellevue back to Renton.
beats.reasoning: 45 min drive home; Derek leaves the site in mid-afternoon to beat evening traffic and get home to family.

beats.activity: Dinner with family, watch evening news, relax on the porch in sunshine.
beats.reasoning: Derek unwinds at home; sunny Monday evening perfect for outdoor relaxation before bed, no baseball coaching on weekdays.
7.5 hours sleep, ending at 5:30am for Tuesday's early 6am work start; Derek maintains consistent sleep schedule.

diary: Started the day early like always—5:30 AM coffee hit different this morning, maybe because the forecast said sun all day. Got to the Bellevue site by 6:45 and grabbed breakfast at Mel's; the crew was already texting about the World Cup match at 11. Couldn't resist slipping away to The Tap House for the first half of USA vs Wales. The place was packed for 10am on a Monday, which was wild. Got back to the site feeling a little guilty but energized, honestly. The concrete pour went smooth, and the framing inspection showed solid progress. Grabbed a Jimmy John's sandwich at lunch and just sat in the sun for a bit—haven't done that in weeks. By 2pm I was wrapping up the daily log and heading home. Dinner with the family was quiet and good. Sat on the porch after with a beer, watching the light change. Felt like I actually lived today instead of just pushing through it.
thought_process: I knew the World Cup match was happening, and I kept thinking about it all morning. Usually I'd skip it, but the bars were buzzing early and something about that sunny Monday made me want to bend my own rules a little. I figured an hour wouldn't hurt—the crew had things under control, and honestly, I needed the break. Coming back to coordinate the concrete pour felt grounded again, like I'd scratched an itch. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, but I noticed the traffic was lighter than usual. By afternoon my mood had shifted; I wasn't just managing the day, I was present in it. The sun probably helped. Getting home early enough to actually sit with my family instead of collapsing made a difference too.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
